title: Validate analytical policies against private evidence
description: Evaluate candidate formula, sufficiency, explanation, threshold, severity, and ordering policies over ignored owner-only time-separated evidence before activation or plan approval. Goal trace: G3, G5, and G7.
rationale: A deterministic analytical rule can still be consistently misleading; evidence, not plausibility, earns release scope.
story_want: validate candidate insights against representative private periods and counterexamples
story_so_that: unsupported or falsely severe signals cannot enter the released catalogue
acceptance_criteria: Given a candidate policy set and owner-only evaluation manifest, when validation starts, then candidate versions, expected-state fingerprint, corpus fingerprint, producer contract versions, window policy, and evaluation-tool version are recorded.

Given a completed validation run, when its result is returned, then every manifest case is accounted for as supported, unavailable, insufficient, correct signal, incorrect signal, correct severity, false severity, or disclosure-canary outcome.

Given sparse, zero, recurring, front-loaded, irregular, refund-heavy, plan-change, category-lifecycle, classification-gap, and correction cases, when required corpus coverage is checked, then every required shape is present or validation fails before policy activation.
Given any false high-severity Attention Item, when candidate activation is evaluated, then the Attention policy is rejected.

Given a candidate Forecast, Trend, or Anomaly without a deterministic formula, explicit comparable baseline, minimum evidence rule, limitations, and named unavailable state, when activation is evaluated, then that signal policy is rejected.

Given a candidate report shape that the owner does not accept as improving a named decision over raw Budget Position inspection, when the plan gate is evaluated, then the do-less kill criterion is reported as triggered.
Given an unavailable private manifest, when existing released policies are used, then ordinary report generation may continue with their unchanged versions.
Given an unavailable private manifest, when a new or changed analytical policy is proposed for release, then activation is blocked.

Given a validation result, when it is persisted or exported, then only fingerprints, structural case identifiers, aggregate outcomes, and canary status are present; filenames, financial values, merchant text, report payloads, and expected rows are absent.

Given the public INSIGHTS capability set, when it is enumerated, then no owner-facing policy create, edit, activate, retire, scripting, or plugin operation is present; candidate validation is a release evidence gate for built-in policies.
security_criteria: Private evidence stays ignored, owner-only, and absent from Git, Lex, process arguments, diagnostics, and ordinary validation output.
